official_functions.role: Decode, write, and observe the official initialize_border body
Define the primitive field codecs used by the official body
Name the packet id assigned in Play clientbound state
Write and dispatch-decode the full official Play clientbound initialize_border frame

invariants: The official Play clientbound table assigns initialize_border to packet id 0x2b.
The official Play clientbound protocol codec dispatches the initialize_border id to ClientboundInitializeBorderPacket.
The official initialize_border body is center X double, center Z double, old size double, new size double, lerp time VarLong, absolute max size VarInt, warning blocks VarInt, and warning time VarInt.
The fixture is decoded by the official STREAM_CODEC from primitive fields, so it does not require initialized WorldBorder, Level, registry contents, or game state.

The framed bytes, packet table id, body bytes, decoded packet class, decoded fields, Play clientbound packet table, and remaining decode byte counts are generated by the official jar-backed answer artifact.
This contract does not prove world-border runtime behavior, world state, render readiness, or client-load completion.
test_topology_note: rust_test_target names the current case adapter only; the manifest owns the runnable test topology.
stop_boundary: Do not use this framed dispatch/decode contract to infer world-border runtime behavior, world state, render readiness, or client-load completion.
